metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate all 7 drift-instance record IDs (or link directly to the section of the 2026-07-03 derisk evaluation that lists them) in Phase 4 so an executor can target them without external lookup.
findings.finding: Phase 4 references '5x archived->Done' drift instances without naming them; only REB-530 and REB-532 are identified. An executing agent cannot target the correct records without their IDs (or a pointer to where the list lives).
findings.location: Phase 4 — live heal under caps
findings.suggested_fix: Add a table or inline list: '5x archived->Done: REB-NNN, REB-NNN, REB-NNN, REB-NNN, REB-NNN; GC: REB-530; adopt: REB-532' — or add a hyperlink anchor to the exact derisk-evaluation section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC #4 — e.g. a unit test that stubs one mutation, arms the blast-radius guard above threshold, and asserts zero mutations were applied plus bridge_alert was emitted; or describe a log-grep on the pass artifact that proves ordering.
findings.finding: AC #4 (circuit breaker) states 'abort BEFORE first mutation, alert emitted' but no proving command or test is described that would verify the abort happens before any mutation rather than after one or more.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC #4; Phase 4 — Wire check_blast_radius

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit test (or describe the existing test) that injects a corrupt/missing baseline into BindingStore and asserts the pass aborts with no mutations applied, mapping it to AC #3's 'corrupt-baseline fail-closed abort preserved' clause.
findings.finding: AC #3 includes 'corrupt-store fail-closed abort preserved' as a required property of the consumer swap, but no test or verification step for this specific failure mode is described in Phase 3 or elsewhere.
findings.location: Phase 3 — consumer swap; Acceptance Criteria — AC #3

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Require the census log excerpts to be committed to the repo (e.g. `docs/evidence/3006-e198-heal-census.txt`) OR attached to the closing PR as a CI artifact, so AC #5 is verifiable from repo history independently of ticket state.
findings.finding: AC #5 requires 'evidence (census log excerpts) attached to this ticket' — this is a one-time human artifact-attachment action, not a durable repo/CI-verifiable end-state, making it unverifiable by an automated reviewer or in a future audit via the repo alone.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC #5
findings.suggested_fix: Add a step: 'Paste census log excerpt into PR description AND commit a trimmed copy to docs/evidence/3006-e198-heal-census.txt so the fixed-point proof survives ticket archival.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in Phase 4 whether bootstrap-strict mode operates under a separate absolute cap (MODE_CAPS=10) that bypasses or pre-satisfies the fraction-based `max_acting_fraction` breaker, and document which mechanism governs the heal pass to prevent the breaker from blocking the intended heal.
findings.finding: Phase 4 bullet 1 introduces a fraction-based circuit breaker (`reconciler.max_acting_fraction`, default 0.10) wired into the apply path, while Phase 4 bullet 2 dispatches `mode=bootstrap-strict (MODE_CAPS=10)` to execute the heal. The plan never clarifies whether bootstrap-strict operates under a separate count-based cap that satisfies or supersedes the fraction-based breaker. If the 7 healing instances exceed 10% of total bound keys, the fraction breaker would fire and abort the very heal it is meant to protect, creating a contradiction between the heal delivery mechanism and the cap enforcement described in the same phase.
findings.location: Phase 4 — live heal under caps (both bullets)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note in Phase 4 stating: 'bootstrap-strict enforces MODE_CAPS=10 as its cap; the fraction-based breaker (max_acting_fraction) is disabled or overridden in bootstrap-strict mode — document this explicitly in the config schema and in Phase 4's dispatch instructions.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a documentation task (e.g., in Phase 4 or as a standalone step) that explicitly assigns writing/updating `docs/jira-sync-setup.md` with the rollback procedure so AC#6 has a corresponding described deliverable.
findings.finding: AC#6 requires the rollback procedure to be documented in `docs/jira-sync-setup.md`, but no section of the plan body describes this documentation work as a deliverable. The Summary mentions the rollback brake conceptually ('ROLLBACK BRAKE (pin to dry-run) at every step below') but no phase owns the task of writing or updating that doc file.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria #6; Summary
findings.suggested_fix: Append to Phase 4: 'Update docs/jira-sync-setup.md: document the workflow_dispatch dry-run pin as the rollback brake, with step-by-step instructions for each phase's rollback trigger.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit Phase 3 back-out procedure specifying: if post-merge divergence is detected, (1) pin workflow mode to dry-run, (2) git-revert the swap commit and redeploy, (3) verify the next dry-run pass reads from prev_snapshot again — and reference this in AC#3 and docs/jira-sync-setup.md.
findings.finding: The rollback path for Phase 3 (consumer swap) is described only as 'pin workflow_dispatch mode input to dry-run.' Pinning to dry-run stops future pass execution but does not revert the already-merged code change that re-points `outbound_fields._local_matches_prev` to `BindingStore.get_baseline`. If post-merge divergence is detected, the implementer must git-revert the swap commit and redeploy — a step the plan neither specifies nor acknowledges.
findings.location: Phase 3 — consumer swap; Summary (ROLLBACK BRAKE)
findings.suggested_fix: In Phase 3, add: 'Back-out: if classifier divergence or direction-preservation failures appear in the first N live passes post-merge, revert the swap commit (git revert <sha>), redeploy, and confirm the subsequent pass census shows 0 divergence against the legacy path before re-attempting the swap.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Create or amend an ADR (e.g., extend ADR 0026 or open ADR 0030) documenting the consumer-swap decision: rationale for moving from prev_snapshot to BindingStore.get_baseline, the degrade/fail-closed contract, and the condition under which prev_snapshot can be removed.
findings.finding: Phase 3 introduces what the plan itself calls 'the ONE high-risk seam' — re-pointing outbound_fields from prev_snapshot to BindingStore.get_baseline — but no new or updated ADR is planned to capture this architectural decision. Existing ADR 0026 and ADR 0029 are cited only for semantic-preservation constraints, not updated to record the consumer-swap decision itself.
findings.location: Phase 3 — consumer swap; Acceptance Criteria item 3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a task to Phase 3: 'Update ADR 0026 (or open ADR 0030) to record: (1) consumer swap rationale, (2) absent-baseline->local-wins degrade, (3) corrupt-store->fail-closed abort, (4) prev_snapshot retention condition for L3.' Reference this ADR in the AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add both new config keys (`reconciler.baseline_dual_write`, `reconciler.max_acting_fraction`) — with type, default, and effect — to the project's config reference doc (or the existing docs/jira-sync-setup.md operator section).
findings.finding: Two new public config keys — `reconciler.baseline_dual_write` and `reconciler.max_acting_fraction` (with a default of 0.10) — are introduced but no update to a config reference document or operator guide is planned beyond the inline plan text and the rollback-procedure AC item scoped only to docs/jira-sync-setup.md.
findings.location: Phase 1 (reconciler.baseline_dual_write); Phase 4 (reconciler.max_acting_fraction); Acceptance Criteria item 6
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the docs/jira-sync-setup.md 'Configuration' section (or equivalent) with a table row per key: name, type, default, effect, and when to change it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the failure policy for `BindingStore.set_baseline` write errors in Phase 1: either (a) treat a write failure as a divergent pass (M > 0) so it does not count toward the 10-pass gate, or (b) emit a bridge_alert and abort the pass before incrementing the clean-pass counter.
findings.finding: Phase 1 does not specify what happens when the BindingStore.set_baseline write fails (store unavailable, timeout, or exception): the pass may silently skip the baseline write and continue, producing a false 'clean pass' count toward the 10-pass gate or leaving stale baseline data without an alert.
findings.location: Phase 1 — baseline dual-write shadow
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap the set_baseline call in a try/except; on any store exception, emit a bridge_alert with the error, count the pass as non-clean (equivalent to M>0), and log `baseline_shadow_check` with `equal=0, divergent=ERROR` so operators can distinguish store errors from data divergence.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a RETIRE path for BindingStore: on each pass, delete (or tombstone with TTL) entries for keys present in the store but absent from the current pass's `curr snapshot`, and document this in the Phase 1 spec.
findings.finding: The plan has no RETIRE/cleanup path for BindingStore entries: keys that are unbound (issue deleted, scope changed, or reconciler config narrowed) will accumulate in the store indefinitely, and the divergence check will compare stale baseline values for keys no longer in scope.
findings.location: Phase 1 — baseline dual-write shadow; Phase 3 — consumer swap
findings.suggested_fix: After writing baseline for all current bound keys, call `BindingStore.delete_baseline(key)` for any key in `BindingStore.list_keys()` that is not in the current pass's bound-key set, and log the count of retired keys in the `baseline_shadow_check` record.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify concurrency safety for `BindingStore.set_baseline`: either (a) add a GitHub Actions `concurrency:` group to serialize reconciler workflow runs, or (b) require BindingStore writes to be transactional/CAS per key so concurrent writes are detected and the pass fails-closed rather than producing a mixed-snapshot baseline.
findings.finding: The plan does not address concurrency safety for BindingStore writes when two reconciler runs overlap (e.g., a manually dispatched workflow_dispatch run coinciding with the scheduled 20-min cron run): two simultaneous set_baseline calls for the same key set could interleave, producing a partially-written baseline that neither run owns.
findings.location: Phase 1 — baseline dual-write shadow; Deployment context (workflow_dispatch)
findings.suggested_fix: Add `concurrency: group: reconciler-live\n  cancel-in-progress: false` to reconcile-bridge.yml so that a manual dispatch queues behind (rather than races with) the scheduled run, and document this in the rollout procedure.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test AC for the circuit-breaker TRIP path: construct a pass artifact whose acting-decision count exceeds `max_acting_fraction` and assert (a) zero mutations are applied, (b) a bridge_alert is emitted, and (c) the pass aborts before the first mutation — this must be a behavioral test, not a live execution that stays under cap.
findings.finding: The circuit breaker abort path — 'a pass whose acting decisions exceed max_acting_fraction aborts BEFORE any mutation' — introduces new safety-critical logic whose TRIP behavior (cap exceeded → abort) has no specified test. The only validation in the plan is running the live heal under bootstrap-strict caps, which is expected to stay UNDER the cap and therefore never exercises the breaker's abort branch.
findings.location: Phase 4 — live heal under caps / AC #4
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC #4: 'A unit test or integration test injects a pass with acting_pct > 0.10 and asserts: no mutations applied, bridge_alert fired, abort before first write. This test must run in CI.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to AC #3 an explicit test for the absent-baseline → local-wins degrade: after the consumer swap, verify that a key with no stored baseline causes the consumer to fall back to local-wins rather than fail-closed or error.
findings.finding: The absent-baseline → local-wins degrade path is listed as a PRESERVE requirement in Phase 3 but has no corresponding test specified anywhere in the plan. AC #3 covers only the corrupt-baseline fail-closed case, leaving the absent-baseline branch untested after the consumer swap makes it the live default.
findings.location: Phase 3 — consumer swap / AC #3
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the RED-first test suite (Phase 3) with a case where BindingStore.get_baseline returns absent/None for a tested key and assert the outbound result equals the local value (local-wins), distinct from the corrupt-store abort case.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `decision_census` (and `binding_walk_census`) should emit to stderr/stdout or a separate sink in cap-0 modes, and if so, extend `_NoOpSyncLogger` to a `_StderrCensusLogger` (or equivalent) so the 'every pass, in every mode' invariant is actually satisfiable; otherwise tighten the AC to 'every live/bootstrap pass' to match what the code can deliver.
findings.finding: Phase 2 mandates 'every pass, in every mode' decision_census emission via sync_logger, but the existing _NoOpSyncLogger (reconcile.py:553-565, 710) swallows ALL log events in cap-0 modes (dry-run, reconcile-check). The binding_walk_census event added by the foundation already disappears silently in dry-run (run_differs.py:519). The plan proposes a new census log event with an 'every mode' invariant but does not address the pre-existing suppression gate, making the invariant undeliverable without extending or replacing the no-op logger. This is cargo-cult application of the epic's 'every mode' AC mandate without verifying the existing logging architecture supports it.
findings.location: Phase 2 — shadow consumer + divergence alert; AC line 2 ('decision_census emitted every pass in every mode'); reconcile.py:553-565 (_NoOpSyncLogger), reconcile.py:710 (logger selection), run_differs.py:519 (binding_walk_census already swallowed in dry-run)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify (or add) a `bridge_alert` emission in the circuit-breaker refusal path of `binding_walk.py` (and the planned apply-path gate) so that AC#4's 'alert emitted' clause is satisfied — the current `result.refused = True` path emits nothing.
findings.finding: AC#4 asserts 'alert emitted' when the circuit breaker trips, but the current binding_walk.py breaker (the only check_blast_radius wiring in the codebase) only sets result.refused=True and returns — no bridge_alert is filed on refusal. The plan must add the bridge_alert call explicitly or the AC will silently pass without operators being notified.
findings.location: Phase 4 / AC line 4: 'abort BEFORE first mutation, alert emitted'
findings.suggested_fix: In binding_walk.py, after setting result.refused=True, call binding_store._append_alert (or equivalent) with a tag like 'blast_radius_exceeded' and the breaker.reason. Mirror the same call in the new apply-path gate wired in Phase 4.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the phrase 'Run the classifier in REPORT mode' in Phase 2 with the concrete call — e.g. 'call `classify_mod.classify()` over each binding, collect the resulting Decisions without dispatching mutations, and compare the acting set to what the legacy path planned' — since classify.py has no REPORT mode parameter.
findings.finding: Phase 2 says 'Run the classifier in REPORT mode against the same pass artifacts' but `classify.py` has no `REPORT` mode parameter or concept — decisions have an `is_acting` property and there is a separate offline consumer (bridge_fsck), but no callable 'REPORT mode' switch. An implementor following the plan literally will not find a `REPORT` mode to invoke.
findings.location: Phase 2: 'Run the classifier in REPORT mode against the same pass artifacts'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `reconcile.py` (`_apply_mutations`) or `run_differs.py` as an explicit edit target for Phase 4 and specify how the full `ctx.mutations` list (not just binding_walk mutations) passes through `check_blast_radius` before any leaf applier fires.
findings.finding: `check_blast_radius` is currently wired only inside `binding_walk.py` (covering off-snapshot drift-class A/C mutations). The plan's Phase 4 AC says 'abort BEFORE first mutation' for ALL acting decisions, but the legacy snapshot-diff mutations from `run_differs.py` / `outbound_differ.py` flow into `_apply_mutations` (reconcile.py) without passing through the breaker. Wiring the breaker 'into the apply path' to cover the full mutation set requires a new call site in `reconcile.py` (`_apply_mutations`) or `run_differs.py`, but neither file is listed as an edit target for Phase 4.
findings.location: Phase 4 — 'Wire check_blast_radius into the apply path'; AC line 4
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `check_blast_radius(ctx.mutations, total_bindings, max_acting_fraction)` call at the top of `_apply_mutations` in `reconcile.py`, after the filter step but before `applier.apply`, and emit a `bridge_alert` + return early if `not breaker.allowed`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'REPORT mode' in Phase 2 with the concrete call intended (e.g., 'call `classify.classify()` over the same pass artifacts in read-only fashion and compare the resulting acting set to the mutations the legacy path planned') so the edit target is an unambiguous call site rather than a non-existent mode constant.
findings.finding: Phase 2 refers to 'Run the classifier in REPORT mode' as if `REPORT` is a recognized mode or callable state, but `classify.py` has no `REPORT` mode enum value and `mode.py`'s `Mode` enum has no such member. The term is informal but the plan does not tie it to a concrete call (e.g., 'call `classify.census()` and compare its acting set to the mutations the legacy path planned') — leaving the implementor without a clear symbol to target.
findings.location: Phase 2 — 'Run the classifier in REPORT mode against the same pass artifacts'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'after the existing baseline-advance at pass end' with the precise symbol/location — e.g., 'after `binding_store.seed_baselines_from_snapshot(curr_snapshot)` returns in `reconcile.py`'s pass tail, or as a post-step in `_apply_mutations`' — so the insertion point is unambiguous.
findings.finding: Phase 1 says to insert the dual-write `BindingStore.set_baseline` call and `SyncLogger.log('baseline_shadow_check', ...)` in `reconcile.py` 'after the existing baseline-advance at pass end', but no 'baseline-advance' hook, event, or function by that name exists in `reconcile.py`. The actual baseline-seeding path is `binding_store.seed_baselines_from_snapshot` (called from `binding_store.py:343`), which is invoked from `run_differs.py` or `reconcile.py` indirectly. The insertion point in `reconcile.py` is under-specified.
findings.location: Phase 1 — 'after the existing baseline-advance at pass end' in reconcile.py

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify Phase 4 breaker wiring scope: if the intent is a global guard over all acting mutations (not just the binding-walk), specify the new call site (e.g., in `_apply_mutations()` in `reconcile.py` over the full `ctx.mutations` list before dispatching to `applier.apply()`) and which mutation types count as acting for that check.
findings.finding: Phase 4 says 'Wire check_blast_radius into the apply path... abort BEFORE any mutation' but binding_walk.py already implements this breaker for the binding-walk population (lines 194–206), while applier.apply() has no global blast-radius check. The outbound field-update mutations from compute_outbound_mutations — potentially the largest population — are never gated by the breaker. If Phase 4 only wires the already-existing binding_walk breaker, the AC is trivially satisfied by foundation work; if it intends a global check at the apply boundary over all mutation types, the plan does not specify the new call site or what mutation types count as 'acting' for the global check.
findings.location: Phase 4 — live heal under caps; AC line 4
findings.suggested_fix: In reconcile.py `_apply_mutations`, before the `applier.apply()` call, collect all outbound create/update/delete typed Mutations, run `check_blast_radius` over them (as the acting set), and if `not verdict.allowed` emit a bridge_alert and raise a RuntimeError to abort the pass before any Jira write.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Revise Phase 2 to define a divergence comparison over a shared population: e.g., for every ACTIVE bound pair where the classifier returns SYNC_FIELDS, compare whether `outbound_fields._diff_fields` (legacy) emits any field mutations — a mismatch means the classifier and legacy path disagree on that pair's field state, which is the actual pre-swap validation signal needed.
findings.finding: Phase 2's divergence comparison between 'the classifier's acting set' and 'the acting mutations the legacy path planned' targets structurally disjoint populations: by construction (run_differs.py:497 filters out active_local_ids from the binding walk), the binding-walk classifier operates exclusively on off-snapshot bound pairs (locally archived/deleted tickets absent from `rebar list`), while the legacy differ (differ.compute_mutations) operates on snapshot-visible active pairs. These two sets never overlap, so the proposed divergence alert will never fire and provides no signal before the Phase 3 consumer swap.
findings.location: Phase 2 — shadow consumer + divergence alert

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `seed_baselines_from_snapshot(prev_snapshot)` is called once at Phase 1 start (covering off-snapshot / archived bindings) in addition to the per-pass `set_baseline` from `curr_snapshot`; if not, explicitly acknowledge the local-wins degradation for class-A/C bindings post-Phase 3 swap and confirm it is acceptable.
findings.finding: Phase 1 seeds baselines only from `curr_snapshot` (the active fetch window) on each pass, leaving pre-existing off-snapshot bindings — exactly the drift-class A and C population (archived/deleted local tickets that have left `rebar list`) — with no baseline seeded before the Phase 3 consumer swap. The plan never explicitly states that `seed_baselines_from_snapshot(prev_snapshot)` is called as a one-shot bootstrap (which would cover these entries), nor does it acknowledge that the off-snapshot population silently degrades to local-wins post-swap.
findings.location: Phase 1 — baseline dual-write shadow; AC line 1 ('Dual-write shadow behind reconciler.baseline_dual_write')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-shot call to `binding_store.seed_baselines_from_snapshot(prev_snapshot)` at the start of Phase 1 (guarded by `reconciler.baseline_dual_write=true`). This seeds baselines for all 613 bound+present keys including those only in `prev_snapshot` but no longer in `curr_snapshot` (drift-class A/C population), ensuring the direction-preservation property holds for those pairs after Phase 3 swap. The call is idempotent (safe to re-run).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define what 'REPORT mode' means for the classifier in Phase 2: either add a boolean/enum `acting_only` parameter to the classify wrapper, or name a specific function/protocol (e.g. `census()` over the binding-walk result) that constitutes 'the classifier in report mode' and specify exactly what acting-set comparison is logged as divergence.
findings.finding: Phase 2 references running the classifier in 'REPORT mode' as a named protocol, but no REPORT mode exists in classify.py or anywhere in the codebase. The classifier (classify.py:classify()) is a pure function with no mode parameter — it always produces a Decision with no concept of acting vs. reporting.
findings.location: Phase 2 — shadow consumer + divergence alert

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the `baseline_shadow_check` event schema: define whether comparison uses shape-tolerant field-equality (matching _local_matches_prev semantics), what `keys` contains (divergent keys only), and where in reconcile.py the comparison loop is inserted (after set_baseline at pass end, before or after prev_snapshot advance).
findings.finding: The `baseline_shadow_check` log event specified in Phase 1 is not implemented anywhere in the codebase, and its comparison protocol — what is compared to what, for which keys — is not specified co-located with the log schema.
findings.location: Phase 1 — baseline dual-write shadow; AC#1
findings.suggested_fix: Use `_local_matches_prev` semantics (the same shape-tolerant comparisons in outbound_fields.py) for the baseline-vs-prev comparison; emit `keys` as the list of divergent Jira keys only; insert the loop in `_persist_and_log` after `binding_store.save()` and before `shutil.copy2(curr_path, prev_path)`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the GATE counter's storage (e.g. a `baseline_shadow_clean_streak` integer on the binding store or a dedicated bridge_state file), its reset rule (reset to 0 on any M>0 pass; increment by 1 on M==0), and how Phase 3's merge gate reads it (e.g. a CI check or a named function that returns True when streak >= 10).
findings.finding: The Phase 1 GATE counter (≥10 consecutive clean passes with M==0) has no specified storage location, reset protocol, or enforcement mechanism — it is referenced as a pre-condition for Phase 3 but its state machine is not defined anywhere in the plan.
findings.location: Phase 1 — GATE condition; AC#1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how decision_census is emitted in non-persist (dry-run/reconcile-check) modes: either (a) extend _NoOpSyncLogger to print census to stdout/stderr, (b) emit the census via a separate path (e.g. print to stderr in all modes), or (c) narrow the AC to 'every persist-mode pass' and document the dry-run exception explicitly.
findings.finding: Phase 2 requires `decision_census` to be emitted 'every pass in EVERY mode' via sync_logger, but reconcile.py assigns `sync_logger = _NoOpSyncLogger()` in non-persist modes (dry-run, reconcile-check), silently swallowing the census emit in those modes.
findings.location: Phase 2 — shadow consumer + divergence alert; AC#2
findings.suggested_fix: In _NoOpSyncLogger.log(), forward calls whose event_type is 'decision_census' to print(json.dumps(entry), file=sys.stderr) so no-write passes still surface the census to the GHA run log.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
